title: Dimatar's Big Day at Ginger Catasorry
summary: When Isabella visits her dragonet friend Dimatar, she brings him a fish-flavored cupcake and news that he can now attend Ginger Catasorry. This specialized school, for cats and dragonets, offers a full curriculum including world geography on a planet map, drawing lessons, and bead counting. Dimatar makes friends with a soft gray cat named Ginger over snacks of fish and kibble, sings songs during circle time, and explores a playground equipped with specialized wing-rails. After a full day of learning and fun, Dimatar is picked up by his mom, having successfully completed his first day of school.

highlightQuote.text: Nah, I brought you a fish flavored cupcake with fishy flavored sprinkles on top.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right, let's put the flashlight here. Put the lantern on top.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Dad. No, on the phone light.
Yep. I did it. And turn out the lamp. And the stars come on.

transcript.text: Maybe we could do Seeker visits Ginger's School.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Seeker visits Ginger's School. Now once upon a time...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, it's an Isabella and Seeker story.
Oh. Okay. Do you want to start it?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. Once upon a time Isabella was walking to Dimatar's and she pressed the doorbell. Ding dong.
Who is it? said the voice of a dragonet.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Isabella.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, my old friend Isabella. Come on in. Did you bring me anything from Sophie's bakery?
Nah, I brought you a fish flavored cupcake with fishy flavored sprinkles on top.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Wow, I've never had fishy flavored sprinkles. Can I have it right now please? Nom nom nom. And Dimatar gobbled it up.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And I got very important exciting news.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You do? What's that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And you can go to Ginger school now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ginger school?
This is Ginger school and I'm always the teacher there. I'm going to get out...
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Now, now wait a minute. What kind of school is this? What do I have to learn there?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Ginger Catasorry.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it's a Catasorry school. Wait a minute, what does that even mean?
That means it's a school for cats and dragonets. There's a lot of dragonet students here. And also some cats named Pixie, Pumpkin, Figaro, and Katsumi. Those are the cats and also Ginger and Jay.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: That's a lot of cats.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And Alexander.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And where is this school?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: In my room.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Okay, let's go. I'll give it a try. And what am I going to learn at this school? I guess I'll find out. What do I do first?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's do a planet map. Dad, can you... where's my little flashlight? Do you know where that is?
It's not in your shelf here? Oh, yes it is. Here you go.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Dimatar, this is a... Dimatar, this is a...
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: What's that? says Dimatar.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: So... Asia, Africa, North... South America, Europe and finally Australia.
I've never heard of any of these places, says Dimatar. I just live on the Perfectly Circular Island.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Well... Well, that's a planet map.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's the planet map. Okay. Do you want to try it yourself?
Well, there's no circular island on here, but you said Asia, Africa, North and South America, Europe and finally Australia. Wow, I learned it! Thank you.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: So now you try it. Just hold it down and I'll sing the names as you point. Asia, Africa, North and South America, Europe and finally Australia.
Wow, you're good at that. Well, that was one lesson, says Dimatar. What else am I going to learn here at Catasorry?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: This is the drawing table. Someone forgot to erase it.
The drawing table. What are you going to draw on there?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I'm going to give you a lesson. Here we go, here's the pen.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Here I can hold the flashlight, says Dimatar.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay, thanks. But that's the teacher flashlight.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay, you better hold it then, teacher. Oh, is it a picture of a duck?
Yeah.

Dad
transcript.text: Oh. What's the duck's name?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dizley. Oh, I like that duck. And what are all those dots?
They're his teeth.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: His teeth. Of course. What's next?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Then you put the pen in the pen holder.
Mhm. It's hard to erase. Here, I'll do it. Okay. Now you try Dimatar.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Dad
transcript.text: What should I draw?
Anything you want.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Could you... hold the light up please? I'll draw... Oh, could you hold it higher up and point it down? Not in my eyes, please. Oh my goodness. Can you make it so it lights up the drawing area while I'm drawing? Thank you. That's what I wanted.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What are you drawing? What are you drawing Dimatar?
I'm trying to draw... a little dragon, like me. Dragonet. See there's its mouth with the teeth and a little eye and wings and arms and legs and a tail. That's my dragonet picture.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, then can you erase it?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Okay. Put the pen in the pen holder. Leave it at there first and put the pen in the pen holder. Now slide it all the way down and erase. Okay, it's erased.

transcript.text: Now what do you want to do?

Dad
transcript.text: So far at Catasorry we did planet map and a drawing lesson. There must be lots of other Catasorry activities.
There's lots more at Catasorry.

transcript.text: Oh. I'm feeling a little hungry though. Does Catasorry have snack time?

transcript.text: Oh, kibble and fish for the cats and the dragonets.

Oh, I eat fish, says Dimatar.

transcript.text: Okay, so then you can have the fish bowl, but leave some for the cats, okay? And then the cats will leave some kibble. It's going to be a new food for you, so you can try that at snack time. It's snack time now.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad

transcript.text: Ginger. And Ginger says: I'll sit with you. What's your name?

Oh, oh my gosh, your tail is so soft. Want to feel my tail?

Dad

Scaly and brown.

Crunch crunch crunch munch munch munch, chew swallow.

transcript.text: You like that kibble, huh Ginger?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. And I'm going to eat some fishies, nom nom nom.
Oh, wait! Save some for me, says Dimatar. That's all I really eat.

Izzy
transcript.text: I saved some. I just ate three.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. And then that was the end of snack time.
And then it's time to do circle called the teacher.

Dad
transcript.text: Okay.

Oh yeah, gotta put away the planet map.

Izzy

The sun came out this morning...

transcript.text: And chased the clouds away...
And kissed my friends...

transcript.text: And kissed my friends on the nose. Said how do you do today.

transcript.text: How do you do today.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Izzy
transcript.text: You're my sunshine, my only sunshine, you make me happy when skies are gray. You'll never know, dear, how much I love you, so please don't take my sunshine away.

transcript.speaker: Dad

Now it's playground time.

transcript.text: Oh, I don't have one, what do I do?
No, pick your placemat from this cart, says the teacher.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, okay, I'll pick one. I'll take that one. Thank you, says Dimatar.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: See, everyone's picking from the cart. So now, now, get your lunchbox.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, wait a minute, I didn't bring one, says Dimatar. I didn't know I was supposed to. What do I do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy

On what days?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: On Buy-days.

Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Buy-days? Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but we got fish we can do on music Wednesdays. They're for regular days and music Wednesdays. I'll give you fish to nibble.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you, says Dimatar.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There's kibble. No, thank you, says Dimatar. There's kibble for Ginger. Ginger has a lunchbox, yeah. And so all the dragonets and all the cats ate their lunches.
And then what did they do?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, more work, says Dimatar. This Catasorry school is a lot of work, but I like learning. What's the next piece of work for me to learn about?
Let's count the beads over here.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, I love those shiny sparkly beads. I need all of those. Can I take them home?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No, you can't. They're school work, they stay at school. They are shiny. One, two, three, four... while you're doing this work, can you hold your flashlight like that?
Do you want me to hold it?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No, I can hold it. I'll hold it like that. And how many beads have you got? One, two, three, four, five, six, seven.
Seven beads, says Dimatar. Not bad.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: One, two, three, four, five... one, two, three, four, five, six... one, two, three, four... one, two, three, four. Now you count them.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, my turn says Dimatar. Okay. And you hold the flashlight down. Where should I start with? I'll do green maybe.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, start with light pink.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, no! You don't do that. You count one, one at a time.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: One, two, three, four, five... oh wait, there's one more. Five and one more is...
Six.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Got it. Six light pink ones. And then there's the blue. One blue one. Two, three... three blue ones. Four blue ones. Now do pink ones. Now they'll do the pink ones. Seven pink ones.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Let's see. One...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, seven light pink ones.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I only see six light pink ones.
Wait, let's count. One, two, three, four, five. There's two groups of three there. One, two, three, four, five, six. Three and three make six. I'm going to finish the dark pink ones.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Seems like that's going to be what, five? Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, six.
Oh, excuse me, I missed the last one there. Six dark pink ones.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Now do green ones.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: One, and a two, and a three, and a four, and a five. Five green ones. Now how many rainbow beads are there?
There isn't rainbow. So there's zero rainbow beads. We need a lot more to make the rainbow. More colors to make a rainbow out of beads. That would be fun.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: All right, I liked the bead counting work.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Now it's snack time again.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my goodness, snack time again. More fish I hope.
Yeah, and Ginger sat with Dimatar again. Ate ate ate. And she ate four fish and Dimatar ate the rest and Dimatar munched up the kibble.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: He did? I thought he didn't like the kibble.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, no, I was talking about Ginger. Ginger munched up the kibble.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then what happened next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then it was circle again. Now we're going to say: Am-a-nut, am-a-nut, am-a-nut, am-a-nut. Now we're going to do another song.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You okay? Oh, you found a sticker.
Was on my sock.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: You had a sticker on your sock, that's silly. But it's still circle time, what's the last song we're going to sing?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: The sun came out... the sun came out this morning and chased the stars away. I kissed my friends on the nose. Said how do you do today.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I kissed my friends on the nose. Said how do you do today.
I'm glad, I'm glad the night is over. I'm glad to sing along.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I don't remember the words.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm glad to start the day. I'm glad the day's begun. Hello everybody, hello everyone.
I'm glad the day's begun. Hello everybody, hello everyone.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Now it's out-time until your mom comes.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, says Dimatar. Well, that sounds like fun, maybe I'll fly around a little bit.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, there's a climbing structure, a tower... two towers... and there's a slide. There's steps and a handrail or wing-rail. There's a handrail and a wing-rail and a paw-rail.
That's so convenient for all the different kind of creatures that want to be on the playground.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah, and then when you get to the first tower, you wait, and then when you get to the second one, you go down the slide.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: That sounds like fun, says Dimatar. Let's go. And Dimatar went up and waited and then went down the slide.
All right. I think since we did a whole entire day for Dimatar at Catasorry, that that should be the end of Stars and Stories.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And then Dimatar's mom comes. And Dimatar says: Oh hi mom, let's go home. I'm here to pick you up.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Thank you. I had a great day at Catasorry. Everyone was so nice and I learned a lot and I had delicious food and we sang a song.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Wait, I want to do shadow puppets. Sneaky mama and a bird... hand hawk.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: A hand hawk! Wow.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Dimitar
characters.description: A sharp-witted resident of a circular island castle, this two-century-old purple dragonet carries a pirate’s salt-crusted legacy and a fierce devotion to his salvaged library. Formerly of the Cadasori galleries, Seeker’s grumpy companion now roams the deep to save his ancestral shores, though a recent volcanic disturbance has left him uncharacteristically sickened and frail. Driven by habit and a tireless appetite for fish, he bickers incessantly over snacks while guarding his remaining volumes with a brooding, protective intensity.

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Ginger
characters.description: A soft, round, and silver-coated feline, this exceptionally fluffy educator is one of Minnie and Mickey’s own kittens grown into a figure of quiet authority. Known for daintily distributing napkins with velvet paws and commuting to school behind the wheel, the gray-furred teacher balances his role on Nana’s welcoming committee with a life of polite, helpful service. Whether presiding over his humble sanctuary—the very cage Seeker once coveted—or sustaining himself on crunchy kibble and savory wet food, he remains a steady presence, most recently seen trading his classroom duties for the shadows of a secret closet hideout alongside Garfield.

characters.name: Pixie
characters.description: A nimble operative in Kitty’s elite superhero squad, this scholarly feline balances tactical missions and Ginger Catasorry studies with a profound appreciation for restorative slumber. Currently navigating a perilous underground expedition, the talking adventurer greets each waking moment with a signature "Hello, hello," shifting seamlessly from a quiet nap to the sharp-eyed focus required for subterranean survival.

characters.name: Pumpkin
characters.description: A student of Ginger Catasorry and veteran of Kitty the Superhero’s specialized troop, this feline translates the tactical intuition of dark gopher tunnels into rigorous academic study. Despite their combat experience, they harbor a paralyzing fear of being eaten by their subterranean foes—initially trembling at the mere sight of the big gopher—a dread that frequently sends the scholar scurrying to hide between Izzy’s legs for sanctuary.

characters.name: Figaro
characters.description: A sharp-witted scholar of the Ginger Catasorry, this feline hero balances rigorous academic discipline with the street-wise loyalty of Kitty’s superhero troop. Driven by a restless spirit, they are the first to slip into the night in search of adventure, prowling for action while the rest of the crew sleeps.

characters.name: Katsumi
characters.description: A distinguished scholar of the Ginger Catasorry and elite member of the academy’s talking cat crew, this feline has emerged from a long dormancy to join Kitty’s cat troop. Balancing studious discipline with silver-tongued eloquence, they bring the full weight of their feline intellect and the rare gift of speech to the unfolding journey.

characters.name: Jay
characters.description: A cat student at Ginger Catasorry.

characters.name: Alexander
characters.description: A poised driver and student at Ginger Catasorry, this feline friend to Dimetar and Scratch Cat manages a digital Tamagotchi empire from the confines of a lunchbox. After recently representing Silver during the playdate, their steady paw was led far from the morning commute into the treacherous depths of the dark forest, where they navigated the shadows alongside Jane.

characters.name: Dizley
characters.description: A duck drawn during a lesson who unexpectedly has teeth.

characters.name: Dimatar's Mom
characters.description: The dragonet's mother who arrives at the end of the day to pick him up.

places.name: Dimatar's Tower
places.description: The dragonet's home where Isabella visits.

places.name: Sophie's Bakery
places.description: A shop known for making delicious treats.

places.name: Cadasori
places.description: A school where both dragonettes and cats attend classes.

places.name: Circular Island
places.description: A perfectly circular landmass rising from the waters of the distant reaches, this remote sanctuary serves as both the private residence of Dimitar and the hallowed grounds of the school.

places.name: The Playground
places.description: This inclusive island recreational area features soaring wing-rails and traditional swings anchored by a grand tower with a vibrant blue slide and ascending stairs. Designed for every species, the school grounds transform during rainfall into a landscape of shimmering puddles where Hattie retreats to play amidst the thoughtfully integrated architecture.
